  ✔ no-color-support › colors can be forced by using chalk.level
  ✔ visible › visible: normal output when level > 0
  ✔ visible › visible: no output when level is too low
  ✔ visible › test switching back and forth between level == 0 and level > 0
  ✖ chalk › don't add any styling when called as the base function 
  ✔ chalk › support multiple arguments in base function
  ✔ chalk › support automatic casting to string
  ✔ chalk › style string
  ✔ chalk › support applying multiple styles at once
  ✔ chalk › support nesting styles
  ✔ chalk › support nesting styles of the same type (color, underline, bg)
  ✔ chalk › reset all styles with `.reset()`
  ✔ chalk › support caching multiple styles
  ✔ chalk › alias gray to grey
  ✔ chalk › support variable number of arguments
  ✔ chalk › support falsy values
  ✔ chalk › don't output escape codes if the input is empty
  ✔ chalk › keep Function.prototype methods
  ✔ chalk › line breaks should open and close colors
  ✔ chalk › line breaks should open and close colors with CRLF
  ✔ chalk › properly convert RGB to 16 colors on basic color terminals
  ✔ chalk › properly convert RGB to 256 colors on basic color terminals
  ✔ chalk › don't emit RGB codes if level is 0
  ✔ chalk › supports blackBright color
  ✔ chalk › sets correct level for chalkStderr and respects it
  ✔ chalk › keeps function prototype methods
  ✔ instance › create an isolated context where colors can be disabled (by level)
  ✔ instance › the `level` option should be a number from 0 to 3
  ✔ level › don't output colors when manually disabled
  ✔ level › enable/disable colors based on overall chalk .level property, not individual instances
  ✔ level › propagate enable/disable changes from child colors
  ✔ level › disable colors if they are not supported
